steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the three interior angles
steps.content: The triangle has three interior angles: one measuring $31^{\circ}$, one measuring $2b^{\circ}$, and one measuring $a^{\circ}$. We're given that $a = 45$, so the third angle is $45^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property and solve for b
steps.content:
The sum of all interior angles in any triangle is $180^{\circ}$. Setting up the equation:
$$31 + 2b + 45 = 180$$
Combine the constants:
$$76 + 2b = 180$$
Subtract 76 from both sides:
$$2b = 104$$
Divide by 2:
$$b = 52$$
A is correct.
